title: Derris marginata
intro: Derris marginata is a plant species commonly found in tropical regions, known for its medicinal and insecticidal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Derris marginata contains bioactive constituents like rotenone, a natural insecticide, as well as flavonoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Derris marginata is often used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Derris marginata is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the genus Derris, which is a group of flowering plants. Derris marginata is native to tropical regions and is often found in moist areas, such as riverbanks and wetlands. The species Derris marginata is further classified into various subspecies based on its geographical distribution. Derris marginata is a member of the order Fabales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants.

Its roots and leaves are often used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to treat skin conditions, such as wounds and ulcers, due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.
